It has been ported and upgraded several times, but it never had a true remake. The Remake from this article is a new indie PC game that wears the skin of its title, an alleged remake of just the final dungeon of an unnamed game that seems to mostly be a riff on Chrono Trigger. The game contains collectible manual pages, developer documentary videos, and voice clips from the devs explaining their thought processes in recapturing the magic of that mysterious RPG.

Despite being met with criticism for its poor quality and tedious gameplay, Donkey Kong 64 has been praised for its ambition, cleverness, and memorable characters, including its cast of characters that were funny and quite memorable. The game's sales figures also suggest it was a commercial success, ranking as the #1 best-selling video game of 1999 in the US market in dollars and exceeding 2.3 million units sold on the N64 version, with $130 million in lifetime sales.
